content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · · Administration · · · Executive/Legal · · · Enforcement · · · Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660              322-5991              322-6441              322-6444


January 30, 1979

79-19

Marilyn Coons
The Berkeley Democratic Club
58 Domingo Avenue
Berkeley, California 94705

Dear Ms. Coons:

Please forgive the long delay in responding to your letter to the
Commission dated July 27, 1978. The letter was misfiled and not
found until you brought it to our attention when you testified
before the Commission in December, 1978. Attached to your letter
you set forth the Berkeley Democratic Club activities, which were
independent of the activities of the separate committee formed by
the Berkeley Democratic Club to receive and make contributions,
the Berkeley Committee. This letter will address obligations of the Berkeley Democratic Club only.

There seems to be some confusion concerning when a committee is
formed under the Political Reform Act. Government Code Section
82013 defines a committee in relevant part as:

"...any person or combination of persons who
directly or indirectly receives contributions or
makes expenditures or contributions for the purpose
of influencing or attempting to influence the action
of the voters for or against the nomination or election of one or more candidates, or the passage or
defeat of any measure, including any committee or
subcommittee of a political party, whether national,
state or local if: (a) contributions received total
five hundred dollars ($500) or more in a calendar
year..."

There are, however, two different types of Section 82013(a) committees. The first type is a primary purpose committee which is
formed or existing primarily for "political purposes" meaning to
exist for the purpose of influencing or attempting to influence
the action of the voters for or against the nomination or election
of a candidate or candidates or the qualification or passage of any
measure. An example of a primary purpose committee is a committee
organized to elect a specific candidate, e.g. Committee to Elect
Brown.

Ms. Marilyn Coons                          -2-                          January 30, 1979


     The second type is a multi-purpose committee which exists for a
variety of purposes, one of which is to make contributions or
expenditures in connection with elections. An example of a multi-
purpose committee is the National Women's Political Caucus which
exists to promote women's causes and may occasionally make contri-
butions to further that purpose. From the facts you presented, it
appears that the Berkeley Democratic Club is a multi-purpose
committee.

     A multi-purpose committee must receive $500 in reportable contribu-
tions before it is required to file a Statement of Organization and
campaign statements. Reportable contributions are payments received
which are "earmarked" for making contributions or expenditures. A
payment is earmarked when, at the time of making the payment, the
donor knows or has reason to know that the payment, or funds with
which the payment will be commingled, will be used to make contribu-
tions or expenditures relative to elections.

     After the Berkeley Democratic Club makes a contribution(s) in any
election, a "history" has been established. Therefore in the next
election for which the Club makes contributions the members have
reason to know that a portion of their dues will be used for politi-
cal purposes and are therefore earmarked. A reliable method for
determining what portion of a dues payment is a contribution is to
allocate on the basis of the amount of money contributed to the
Club. For example, if the Club receives $2,000 in members' dues
and contributes $1,000 to candidates or measures, it will have
received $1,000 in earmarked contributions; and fifty percent (50%)
of each member's dues will be a contribution. Under this system,
once the Club contributes $500 in any election after a history has
been established by the Club's prior political contributions, it
must register as a "recipient committee" and file campaign statements.

     The Club will retain its status as a recipient committee until it
terminates. By regulation (2 Cal Admin. Code §18404) the Commission
has outlined the conditions for a committee to terminate.

     The treasurer must declare that the committee:

     1. Has ceased to receive contributions or make expenditures
        and does not anticipate receiving contributions or making
        expenditures in the future;

     2. has eliminated or has declared that it has no intention
        or ability to discharge all of its debts, loans received
        and other obligations;

     3. has no surplus funds; and

     4. has filed all required campaign statements disclosing all
        reportable transactions.

     Under the conditions of this regulation the Berkeley Democratic
Club would not be able to terminate its status as a committee until
it can declare that it has no intention in the future to make contri-
butions or expenditures from its general fund.

Ms. Marilyn Coons                    -3-                    January 30, 1979


A recipient committee may file either the long (Form 420) or short
(Form 450) campaign statement depending on the activity.

After you receive this letter you may have specific questions.
Please call me collect and I will be happy to give you whatever
assistance is necessary. If you plan to be in Sacramento and
would like to sit down with me and discuss what to do next, let
me know and we can set a time.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
                                        Technical Assistance Program
                                        Coordinator

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

January 30, 1979

79-19

Marilyn Coons
The Berkeley Democratic Club
58 Domingo Avenue
Berkeley, California 94705

Dear Ms. Coons:

Please forgive the long delay in responding to your letter to the
Commission dated July 27, 1978. The letter was mislaid and not
found until you brought it to our attention when you testified

Ms. Marilyn Coons                    -3-                 January 30, 1979


A recipient committee may file either the long (Form 420) or short
(Form 450) campaign statement depending on the activity.

After you receive this letter you may have specific questions.
Please call me collect and I will be happy to give you whatever
assistance is necessary. If you plan to be in Sacramento and
would like to sit down with me and discuss what to do next, let
me know and we can set a time.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
                                        Technical Assistance Program
                                        Coordinator

RSK:bw

Enclosure

cc: Edyth Campbell
    City Clerk, Berkeley

State of California

Memorandum

To : Gina S-K.                                    Date : Dec. 8, 1978

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Lee Rosenthal

Subject: Letter from Marilyn Coons

We received the letter from Mrs. Coons, but it
may not have been xeroxed and routed. We never
responded. I am concerned because I believe that one or
more of her committees should have filed as a recipient
committee and despite Mrs. Coons' attempts to make
the Berkely Democratic Club appear harmless, it
fact one of the most powerful political groups in
that city. Please talk to Mrs. Coons and get them to
file whatever they should have filed as soon as
possible.

LR:plh
Attachment

THE BERKELEY DEMOCRATIC CLUB

58 DOMINGO AVENUE, BERKELEY, CALIFORNIA 94705
FOUNDED IN 1934

President
Noel Nellis

Vice President
Wm. T. "Zack" Brown

Secretary
Jeffrey Newman

Treasurer
Marjean Coons

Ruth Blunn
Taylor Culver
Patrick Devaney
Dan Dean
Margaret Gee
Edward Kallgren
Richard Scott
Betty Weekes
Harry Weininger
Uve Wittstock

JUL 31 | 36PM '78

July 27, 1978

Fair Campaign Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

Semi Annual Report 2/1/78 to 6/30/78

Gentlemen:

As Treasurer of the Berkeley Democratic Club
I would like to report a campaign expenditure for which
there doesn't seem to be an appropriate form.

The Berkeley Democratic Club endorsed 12 candidates
in the June 6th Primary election. They were:

Tom Bates - 12th Assembly District
Elihu Harris - 13th Assembly District
Judge Jacqueline Taber - Alameda County Superior
Court

Robert Coney - Alameda County Superintendent of
Schools
William Henrikson - Alameda County Treasurer-Tax
Collector

Alameda County Democratic Central Committee
Candidates for District 5:
    Booker T. Jackson, Jr.
    Ed Kallgren
    William Byron Rumford, Sr.
    Clarence Bailey
    Patrick Devaney
    Andrea Washburn
    Harry Dov Weininger

The Club then formed a separate committee to run
the campaign for the Central Committee candidates and
coordinate an effort to combine with the campaigns of
the other endorsed candidates to produce a campaign
piece to mail to all registered Democrats in the 5th
District of Alameda County. The name of this committee
was the Better Democrats Committee; treasurer was
Joyce McReynolds of 571 Neilson St., Berkeley, Ca.
94707; and their identification number is 782075.

The Berkeley Democratic Club made contributions to
the following candidates from the Club's treasury:
    Jacqueline Taber's Campaign 5/24/78 $125.00
    Booker T. Jackson 5/24/78 $125.00

THE BERKELEY DEMOCRATIC CLUB
58 DOMINGO AVENUE, BERKELEY, CALIFORNIA 94705

-2-

Robert Coney Campaign                5/25/78        $125.00
Andrea Washburn                      5/29/78        $200.00
Clarence Bailey                      5/30/78        $200.0.0
Patrick Devaney                      5/31/78        $125.00
Wm. B. Rumford, Sr.                 6/5/78         $100.00
     Since each of these contributions was reported by each
receiving committee, and since no contribution to any
individual candidate was over $500.00, no statement of
organization was filed by the Berkeley Democratic Club.

     After the election the Berkeley Democratic Club held
a fund-raising event (on June 23rd) to help defray the
deficit of the Better Democrats Committee. On June 26th
the Berkeley Democratic Club paid their only outstanding
bill - Chas. Weidner & Co., 77 Jack London Square, Oakland -
(for labels and mailing house) in the amount of $777.45.
The Better Democrats Committee has now ceased its fund
raising efforts and has returned $153.69 of that amount
to the Berkeley Democratic Club which makes our contribution
to that Committee in excess of $500.00. In the interest of
full disclosure and compliance with the Fair Political
Practices Reform Act the Berkeley Democratic Club wishes
to report this expenditure.

     The Berkeley Democratic Club does not always take an
active "reportable" role in every election. When such an
effort is organized it seems best for our purposes to run
the campaign with a separate committee that "dies" after
each election. This way the treasuries are kept separate
and the campaign committee takes' the responsibility for
their own fund raising, campaign reports, etc. Please
advise us if there is an appropriate form for the Berkeley
Democratic Club to use under these circumstances as we have
found this the most convenient way to operate keeping our
occasional campaign efforts separate from our on-going
club activities.

     I might add that this was the reporting procedure we
followed after our Spring election last year using form
450 titled Association Campaign Statement - Short Form
dated 1/27/78, and a letter of explanation. That form
(450) is now titled Recipient Committee Campaign Statement
Short Form which does not seem suitable to the activities
described above.

     You may contact me at the Club's address, which is also
my address: 58 Domingo Ave., Berkeley, Ca. 94705. I will
assume that this letter suffices unless I hear otherwise.

                                        Sincerely yours,
                                        [illegible]
                                        Marylyn Coons, Treasurer
                                        (415) 848-4286
